rulings.Case Title: STEVE RYAN VS. MITCHELL ROSENFELD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 23, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF STEVE RYAN's Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Judgment is DENIED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Third Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production [Set Two], For Further Production, Privilege Log, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Douglass Manor Homeowners Association And Its Counsel Of
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 23, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF STEPHEN KAVANAUGH's Third Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production [Set Two], For Further Production, Privilege Log, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Douglass Manor Homeowners Association And Its Counsel is DENIED. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted against Plaintiff's counsel in the amount of $4,033 to be paid within 10 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Prevailing-Party Attorneys? Fees (Civil Code Section 1942.5 And Crc 3.1702)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 23, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT CHARLES MA's Notice Of Motion For Prevailing-Party Attorneys' Fees (Civil Code Section 1942.5 And Crc 3.1702) is DENIED. Moving party failed to show that this action or any part thereof was "brought for damages for retaliatory eviction." CC 1942.5(i). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHN FARRELL ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 23, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANTS ROQUE HERNANDEZ and ERIC BLACK's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED as follows: action against the moving defendants (cause of action for declaratory relief) shall be stayed until writ is resolved. Within 15 days of the final ruling on the writ, Plaintiff shall file an amended complaint for declaratory relief if any actual controversy still remains unresolved. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
